{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Audit Officer",
  "description": "Nett Pharmacy is a foremost retail pharmacy chain with presence in major cities in Nigeria. At Nett Pharmacy, we provide top-quality and innovative health and beauty products and services that enable our customers to live well and live better.

Nett Pharmacy is a foremost retail pharmacy chain with presence in major cities in Nigeria. At Nett Pharmacy, we provide top-quality and innovative health and beauty products and services that enable our customers to live well and live better.

Our main goal is to support our customers and patients through different stages of their lives and enable them to live healthier lives by providing guidance and information to put them in control of their health. We offer a broad range of product categories from over-the-counter medicines and prescription medications, vitamins and supplements, to medical aids and small diagnostics equipment and a very wide range of skincare products to meet the needs of our esteemed customers.

Our vision is to become the leading retail pharmacy, reputable for providing high quality, affordable medications, skincare products and professional services in Nigeria, by leveraging on technology to enhance our supply chain distribution for improved efficiency and grow our footprints both online and physical locations, to bring us closer to our customers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Audit Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Asaba, Delta
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
As an Audit Officer at Nett Pharmacy, you will be responsible for conducting internal audits to assess the effectiveness of our controls, processes, and risk management practices. Your role will involve:


	Monitoring and implementation of strong inventory controls across all our branches.
	Evaluating the adequacy and effectiveness of internal controls.
	Reviewing financial records, operational processes, and compliance with policies and regulations.
	Identifying areas of improvement and providing recommendations.
	Preparing detailed audit reports and presenting findings to management.
	Monitoring the implementation of audit recommendations.


Key Responsibilities


	Conduct physical inventory counts and reconcile with inventory records on our ERP system
	Evaluate inventory management practices to ensure accurate tracking and minimize shrinkage across all our branches
	Work with the branch managers to ensure accurate inventory controls in all our branches
	Liaise with the warehouse / distribution centre to ensure all inventory is moved according to company policies
	Conduct thorough and objective internal audits of financial and operational activities.
	Assess compliance with internal policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements.
	Analyze data and prepare comprehensive audit reports with actionable recommendations to management.
	Collaborate with various departments to understand processes and identify risks.
	Stay updated on industry best practices and regulatory changes.
	Assist in the development and enhancement of internal audit methodologies.
	Support external auditors during annual audits and other reviews.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
	Minimum 2-3 years of experience as an auditor, preferably in a retail environment, is an added advantage
	Professional certification are an added advantage
	Strong knowledge of auditing principles, practices, and standards.
	Proficiency in audit software and MS Office applications, particularly Excel.
	Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills.
	Strong attention to detail and accuracy.
	Effective communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hr@nettpharmacy.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-04-21",
  "validThrough": "2026-05-01",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "NETT Pharmacy Limited"
    
  },

      "@type": "PostalAddress",

      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },

    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",

}



Audit Officer Job at Nett Pharmacy Limited




        
    

        
        Nett Pharmacy is a foremost retail pharmacy chain with presence in major cities in Nigeria. At Nett Pharmacy, we provide top-quality and innovative health and beauty products and services that enable our customers to live well and live better.

Nett Pharmacy is a foremost retail pharmacy chain with presence in major cities in Nigeria. At Nett Pharmacy, we provide top-quality and innovative health and beauty products and services that enable our customers to live well and live better.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Our main goal is to support our customers and patients through different stages of their lives and enable them to live healthier lives by providing guidance and information to put them in control of their health. We offer a broad range of product categories from over-the-counter medicines and prescription medications, vitamins and supplements, to medical aids and small diagnostics equipment and a very wide range of skincare products to meet the needs of our esteemed customers.

Our vision is to become the leading retail pharmacy, reputable for providing high quality, affordable medications, skincare products and professional services in Nigeria, by leveraging on technology to enhance our supply chain distribution for improved efficiency and grow our footprints both online and physical locations, to bring us closer to our customers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Audit Officer

Job Location: Asaba, Delta
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
As an Audit Officer at Nett Pharmacy, you will be responsible for conducting internal audits to assess the effectiveness of our controls, processes, and risk management practices. Your role will involve:


	Monitoring and implementation of strong inventory controls across all our branches.
	Evaluating the adequacy and effectiveness of internal controls.
	Reviewing financial records, operational processes, and compliance with policies and regulations.
	Identifying areas of improvement and providing recommendations.
	Preparing detailed audit reports and presenting findings to management.
	Monitoring the implementation of audit recommendations.


Key Responsibilities


	Conduct physical inventory counts and reconcile with inventory records on our ERP system
	Evaluate inventory management practices to ensure accurate tracking and minimize shrinkage across all our branches
	Work with the branch managers to ensure accurate inventory controls in all our branches
	Liaise with the warehouse / distribution centre to ensure all inventory is moved according to company policies
	Conduct thorough and objective internal audits of financial and operational activities.
	Assess compliance with internal policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements.
	Analyze data and prepare comprehensive audit reports with actionable recommendations to management.
	Collaborate with various departments to understand processes and identify risks.
	Stay updated on industry best practices and regulatory changes.
	Assist in the development and enhancement of internal audit methodologies.
	Support external auditors during annual audits and other reviews.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
	Minimum 2-3 years of experience as an auditor, preferably in a retail environment, is an added advantage
	Professional certification are an added advantage
	Strong knowledge of auditing principles, practices, and standards.
	Proficiency in audit software and MS Office applications, particularly Excel.
	Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills.
	Strong attention to detail and accuracy.
	Effective communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

	Must have good communication skills and should be able to work under minimal supervision.

Method of Application&nbsp;

Account Executive Job at A&E Logistics




        
    

        
        A&E Logistics is a registered world class logistics company saddled with the task of making delivery seamless and affordable. A&E Logistics majors and not limited to Airfreight & Seafreight, Cargo Handling, Supply Chain Management, Procurement and Customs Clearance.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Executive

Job Location: Oshodi/Isolo - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Requirements
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Applicants are required to have either OND, HND or BSC in Account with 2-3 years of experience in accounting with good knowledge and use of excel and microsoft packages
	Must have good communication skills and should be able to work under minimal supervision.


Salary
N80,000 - N120,000 / Month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidate should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Restaurant Manager Job at Prixair Group




        
    

        
        Prixair Group is a diversified Nigerian Companyheadquartered in Abuja.The Group operates across multiple sectors, including Solid minerals mining, hospitality, Media, supermarkets, laundry and Cleaning services, and restaurants (food services). Through its portfolio of businesses, Prixair focuses on delivering quality products and services while driving innovation, operational excellence, and customer-focused experiences across Nigeria. Guided by its core values of knowledge, integrity, teamwork, innovation, and professionalism, the Group continues to expand its footprint, with a vision to become a global enterprise providing quality services and solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Restaurant Manager
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Gaduwa, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilties


	Oversee day-to-day activities such as opening/closing, food service, cleanliness, and compliance with health and safety standards.
	Schedule, and manage employees (servers, cooks, hosts). Ensure staff deliver high-quality service and work efficiently.
	Handle customer complaints, ensure satisfaction, and maintain a welcoming atmosphere.
	Monitor budgets, control costs, track sales, and maximize profitability. This includes managing inventory and reducing waste.
	Ensure the restaurant follows health regulations, food safety standards, and labor laws.


Qualification


	Interested candidates should possess a Bachelor`s Degree in any relevant field.


Method of Application

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resource Manager (Trucking, Trailer & Logistics Operations)",
  "description": "Favourtie HRguide - Our client, a logistics company, is a growing force in the trailer and truck logistics industry, moving freight across the country with a fleet that never sleeps.

They are recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Human Resource Manager (Trucking, Trailer &amp; Logistics Operations)

Job Location:&nbsp;Apapa, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Work Arrangement: hybrid

About the Role


	Our client is looking for a seasoned HR Manager who understands the pace, pressure, and people dynamics unique to the trucking, logistics, and manufacturing distribution world.
	This is a senior leadership role where you will serve as a true business partner to operations, helping us hire the right people, keep them engaged, and ensure every aspect of our workforce management is compliant, efficient, and forward-thinking.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead end-to-end recruitment for drivers, dispatchers, warehouse staff, and corporate roles
	Manage onboarding, compliance, and regulatory documentation
	Develop and implement retention strategies to reduce high driver turnover
	Oversee performance management and disciplinary procedures across all departments
	Partner with operations leadership to align HR strategy with fleet and logistics goals
	Administer benefits, payroll coordination, and employee relations
	Champion a safety-first culture across the entire organization
	Develop and implement HR strategies and initiatives aligned with the overall business strategy
	Bridge management and employee relations by addressing demands, grievances or other issues
	Support current and future business needs through the development, engagement, motivation and preservation of human capital
	Develop and monitor overall HR strategies, systems, tactics and procedures across the organization
	Oversee and manage a performance appraisal system that drives high performance
	Maintain pay plan and benefits program
	Assess training needs to apply and monitor training programs.


What We&#39;re Looking For


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in HR, Business Administration, or a related field
	3 years of HR management experience in trucking, logistics, manufacturing, or distribution
	Solid understanding of DOT regulations, CDL requirements, and driver compliance
	Proven record of reducing turnover in high-volume, shift-based environments
	Strong knowledge of employment law and multi-state compliance
	Experience with HRIS systems and workforce planning tools
	Excellent interpersonal and conflict resolution skills
	SHRM-CP, SHRM-SCP, or PHR certification preferred.


Important:


	Candidates must have prior HR experience specifically within trucking, logistics, manufacturing, distribution, or a closely related industrial operations environment.
	Applications without relevant industry experience will not be considered.


Why Join Us


	Competitive base salary (N350,000 - N500,000 monthly)&nbsp;commensurate with experience
	Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance
	Paid time off, holidays, and personal leave.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;Foyinoluwa@gmail.com&nbsp;using &quot;HR Manager&quot;&nbsp;as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-27",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

  },

    "@type": "Place",

      "@type": "PostalAddress",

Human Resource Manager (Trucking, Trailer & Logistics Operations) Job at Favourtie HRguide




        
    

        
        Favourtie HRguide - Our client, a logistics company, is a growing force in the trailer and truck logistics industry, moving freight across the country with a fleet that never sleeps.

They are recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Manager (Trucking, Trailer & Logistics Operations)

Job Location: Apapa, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Work Arrangement: hybrid

About the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Our client is looking for a seasoned HR Manager who understands the pace, pressure, and people dynamics unique to the trucking, logistics, and manufacturing distribution world.
	This is a senior leadership role where you will serve as a true business partner to operations, helping us hire the right people, keep them engaged, and ensure every aspect of our workforce management is compliant, efficient, and forward-thinking.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead end-to-end recruitment for drivers, dispatchers, warehouse staff, and corporate roles
	Manage onboarding, compliance, and regulatory documentation
	Develop and implement retention strategies to reduce high driver turnover
	Oversee performance management and disciplinary procedures across all departments
	Partner with operations leadership to align HR strategy with fleet and logistics goals
	Administer benefits, payroll coordination, and employee relations
	Champion a safety-first culture across the entire organization
	Develop and implement HR strategies and initiatives aligned with the overall business strategy
	Bridge management and employee relations by addressing demands, grievances or other issues
	Support current and future business needs through the development, engagement, motivation and preservation of human capital
	Develop and monitor overall HR strategies, systems, tactics and procedures across the organization
	Oversee and manage a performance appraisal system that drives high performance
	Maintain pay plan and benefits program
	Assess training needs to apply and monitor training programs.


What We're Looking For


	Bachelor's Degree in HR, Business Administration, or a related field
	3 years of HR management experience in trucking, logistics, manufacturing, or distribution
	Solid understanding of DOT regulations, CDL requirements, and driver compliance
	Proven record of reducing turnover in high-volume, shift-based environments
	Strong knowledge of employment law and multi-state compliance
	Experience with HRIS systems and workforce planning tools
	Excellent interpersonal and conflict resolution skills
	SHRM-CP, SHRM-SCP, or PHR certification preferred.


Important:


	Candidates must have prior HR experience specifically within trucking, logistics, manufacturing, distribution, or a closely related industrial operations environment.
	Applications without relevant industry experience will not be considered.


Why Join Us


	Competitive base salary (N350,000 - N500,000 monthly) commensurate with experience
	Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance
	Paid time off, holidays, and personal leave.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "HR Manager" as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Marketing Officer (Furniture Outlet)",
  "description": "Prixair Group is a diversified Nigerian Companyheadquartered in Abuja.The Group operates across multiple sectors, including Solid minerals mining, hospitality, Media, supermarkets, laundry and Cleaning services, and restaurants (food services). Through its portfolio of businesses, Prixair focuses on delivering quality products and services while driving innovation, operational excellence, and customer-focused experiences across Nigeria. Guided by its core values of knowledge, integrity, teamwork, innovation, and professionalism, the Group continues to expand its footprint, with a vision to become a global enterprise providing quality services and solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Marketing Officer (Furniture Outlet)
Jopb Location:&nbsp;Gaduwa, Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Marketing Officer is responsible for planning and executing marketing strategies to increase sales of furniture products, enhance brand awareness, and drive customer engagement both online and offline


Responsibilities


	Develop and implement marketing campaigns to promote furniture collections (e.g., sofas, beds, office furniture).
	Conduct market research to identify customer preferences, trends, and competitor activities.
	Manage digital marketing channels such as social media, email campaigns, and website content.
	Coordinate advertising efforts (online ads).
	Generate leads and convert them into customers.

Marketing Officer (Furniture Outlet) Job at Prixair Group




        
    

        
        Prixair Group is a diversified Nigerian Companyheadquartered in Abuja.The Group operates across multiple sectors, including Solid minerals mining, hospitality, Media, supermarkets, laundry and Cleaning services, and restaurants (food services). Through its portfolio of businesses, Prixair focuses on delivering quality products and services while driving innovation, operational excellence, and customer-focused experiences across Nigeria. Guided by its core values of knowledge, integrity, teamwork, innovation, and professionalism, the Group continues to expand its footprint, with a vision to become a global enterprise providing quality services and solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Marketing Officer (Furniture Outlet)
Jopb Location: Gaduwa, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Marketing Officer is responsible for planning and executing marketing strategies to increase sales of furniture products, enhance brand awareness, and drive customer engagement both online and offline


Responsibilities


	Develop and implement marketing campaigns to promote furniture collections (e.g., sofas, beds, office furniture).
	Conduct market research to identify customer preferences, trends, and competitor activities.
	Manage digital marketing channels such as social media, email campaigns, and website content.
	Coordinate advertising efforts (online ads).
	Generate leads and convert them into customers.
	Organize promotions, exhibitions, and showroom events.
	Create promotional materials like brochures, catalogs, and product descriptions.
	Monitor campaign performance and prepare marketing reports.
	Build relationships with suppliers, media, and advertising agencies.
	Maintain brand consistency across all marketing platforms.


Required Skills and Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or related field.
	Proven experience in marketing (experience in furniture, retail, or interior design is a plus).
	Strong understanding of digital marketing tools (SEO, social media ads, email marketing).
	Creativity and ability to design appealing campaigns.
	Good communication and negotiation skills.
	Analytical skills to interpret marketing data and trends.


Method of Application
